steps.label: What we want to prove
steps.explanation: From falsehood (⊥), prove anything (P). This is 'ex falso quodlibet' — from absurdity, anything follows. If your assumptions are contradictory, you can prove literally anything.

steps.label: Apply ⊥-Elim (Ex Falso)
steps.explanation: ⊥-Elimination says: from ⊥, derive any proposition P. This is a foundational rule — it's why contradictions are so dangerous in logic. One contradiction and everything collapses. In Latin: ex falso quodlibet.
